capability: system role + multi-turn template fidelity (per THIS template)

harness.working_tree: WIN2METAL Phase 2 (Bucket A) validation on Apple M4 16GB. Binary is the FROZEN base build /Volumes/Untitled/camelid-base-28f224b (camelid v0.1.7-92-g28f224b, commit 28f224b); no src changes in this branch. Outputs salvaged under qa/capability/mac_*_out/<row>/.
harness.e2e_harness: qa/capability/* Metal harness; outputs under qa/capability/mac_*_out/llama-3.2-1b

evidence.summary: e2e on Metal (qa/capability/system_multiturn_parity.mjs, Apple M4): a system instruction + 2 user turns + 1 prior assistant turn rendered through the Llama 3.2 chat template, decoded greedily. GENERATION PARITY (class-D): Camelid emits a server-sealed camelid.parity-receipt/v1; verify-receipt feeds the exact 53 prompt token ids to llama.cpp 9632 (acd79d603, Metal) /completion — the continuation matches BIT-EXACT (generated tokens (4), text 'Tokyo.', first_divergent_token_index=-1). TEMPLATE FIDELITY: the ONLY divergence from the oracle is the optional live-date 'Cutting Knowledge Date / Today Date' system preamble that llama.cpp injects (camelid 53 prompt tokens vs llama.cpp 73, first diff at index 5) — a non-deterministic, intended cross-engine difference, which is why parity is asserted on the pinned prompt. Same posture as the Windows row, re-confirmed on Metal. Evidence: qa/capability/mac_sm_out/llama-3.2-1b/summary.json, parity-receipt.json, verify.log.
